title: Exact theory of plasmon reflection and transmission in partially gated two-dimensional system
authors: I. M. Moiseenko, D. A. Svintsov
abstract: We develop an exact theory of plasmon scattering at the boundary between gated and ungated regions of a two-dimensional electron system (2DES). Using the Wiener-Hopf technique, we derive analytical expressions for the complex reflection and transmission coefficients of plasmons incident from both sides of the interface. The theory fully accounts for evanescent fields at the gate edge and radiative losses into free-space electromagnetic waves. In the non-retarded limit and for small gate-2DES separation, the reflected plasmon dominates the total electric field, while radiative losses are negligible when plasmon scattering. The amplitudes and phases of the reflection and transmission coefficients for plasmons incident from both sides have a complex dependence from 2DES-gate separation and conductivity of 2DES. Our results provide a rigorous foundation for modeling tunable plasmonic crystals based on 2DES for terahertz detection and modulation.
summary: This paper provides an exact mathematical theory for how plasmons reflect and transmit at the edge of a metal gate in a 2D electron system. It corrects previous models by accounting for radiative losses and complex phase shifts, which is crucial for designing tunable terahertz plasmonic devices.
main_problem: Determining the exact complex reflection and transmission coefficients of 2D plasmons when scattering at the boundary between gated and ungated regions of a 2D electron system.
main_result: The authors derived analytical expressions for complex coefficients that account for evanescent fields and radiative losses, showing that the reflection phase depends strongly on gate-2DES separation and contradicts simpler plane-wave matching models.
method: The Wiener-Hopf technique was used to solve the wave equation for vector potentials, involving Fourier transforms and residue calculus.
model_or_system: A two-dimensional electron system (2DES) partially covered by a perfectly conducting gate at a distance d above the 2DES plane.
key_observables: Complex reflection and transmission coefficients (amplitude and phase), power reflectance, and power transmittance.
important_parameters: Gate-2DES separation (qud), normalized conductivity (eta), non-retarded limit, and small separation limit.
main_assumptions: The conductivity is assumed to be primarily imaginary (low dissipation), and the theory focuses on the non-retarded limit.
figures_summary: Figures show the dependence of reflection/transmission amplitudes and phases on conductivity and separation, highlighting the divergence from the plane-wave matching approximation and the behavior of power reflectance/transmittance.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the scattering problem, defines the physical model and parameters, applies the Wiener-Hopf method to derive exact coefficients, compares results with the plane-wave matching approximation, and discusses implications for plasmonic crystals.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Reﬂection of the ungated plasmon at a gate edge. Amplitudes of the reﬂection coeﬃcient |ruu| (solid curves) and |rpw uu| (dotted curve) (a) and its phase, (the phase arg(rpw uu) = 0 is not shown) (b), both plotted as functions of the normal- ized gate-2DES separation qud. Diﬀerent colors correspond to diﬀerent values of normalized conductivity η, which is as- sumed purely imaginary (η′ ≪η′′). Dashed curves at Fig. (a) correspond show the fully analytical approximation of the reﬂectance using absolute values of the factorized functions (25), (26).

FIG. 2. Transmission of the ungated plasmon under the gate. Amplitudes of the transmission coeﬃcient |tug| (solid curves) and |t(pw) ug | (dotted curve) (a) and its phase (b), both plot- ted as functions of the normalized gate-2DES separation qud. Diﬀerent colors correspond to diﬀerent values of normalized conductivity η, which is assumed purely imaginary (η′ ≪η′′). Dashed curves at Fig. (a) correspond show the fully analyti- cal approximation of the reﬂectance using absolute values of the factorized functions (25), (26).

FIG. 4. Transmission of the gated plasmon to ungated 2DES. Magnitude of the reﬂection coeﬃcient |tgu| (solid curves) and |tpw gu | (dotted curve) (a) and its phase (b), both plotted as functions of the normalized gate-2DES separation qud. Dif- ferent colors correspond to diﬀerent values of normalized con- ductivity η, which is assumed purely imaginary (η′ ≪η′′). Dashed curves at Fig. (a) correspond show the fully analyti- cal approximation of the reﬂectance using absolute values of the factorized functions (25), (26).

FIG. 3. Reﬂection of the gated plasmon at a gate edge. Am- plitude of the reﬂection coeﬃcient |rgg| (solid curves) and |rpw| (dotted curve) (a) and its phase (b), both plotted as functions of the normalized gate-2DES separation qud. Dif- ferent colors correspond to diﬀerent values of normalized con- ductivity, η′′, which is assumed purely imaginary (η′ ≪η′′). Dashed curves at Fig. (a) correspond show the fully analyti- cal approximation of the reﬂectance using absolute values of the factorized functions (25), (26).

FIG. 5. (a) The power reﬂectance Ruu and transmittance Tug of ungated plasmon(a) and the power reﬂectance Rgg and transmittance Tgu of gated plasmon (b) both plotted as func- tions of the normalized gate-2DES separation qud. Sum of the power reﬂectance and transmittance shown by solid curves. Diﬀerent colors correspond to diﬀerent values of normalized conductivity η, which is assumed purely imaginary (η′ ≪η′′).
